model_parameters.label: Prompt Tools (Functions)
model_parameters.helpText: A list of tools the model may call.

model_parameters.label: Temperature
model_parameters.helpText: What sampling temperature to use, between 0 and 2. Higher values like 0.8 will make the output more random, while lower values like 0.2 will make it more focused and deterministic.

model_parameters.label: Max Tokens
model_parameters.helpText: The maximum number of tokens that can be generated in the Result.

model_parameters.label: Image Detail
model_parameters.helpText: By controlling the detail parameter, you have control over how the model processes the image and generates its textual understanding. You can override this value at attachment level.

model_parameters.label: Top P
model_parameters.helpText: An alternative to sampling with temperature, called nucleus sampling, where the model considers the results of the tokens with top_p probability mass. So 0.1 means only the tokens comprising the top 10% probability mass are considered.

model_parameters.label: Response Format
model_parameters.helpText: The format of the response. If set to 'json', the response will be a JSON object with the keys 'choices' and 'logprobs'. If set to 'text', the response will be a string with the completion text. If set to 'Structured output', the response will match the supplied JSON schema.

subFields.label: JSON Schema
subFields.helpText: A JSON schema that the model will follow when generating the response.

model_parameters.label: Frequency Penalty
model_parameters.helpText: Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalize new tokens based on their existing frequency in the text so far, decreasing the model's likelihood to repeat the same line verbatim.

model_parameters.label: Stop
model_parameters.helpText: Custom text sequences that will cause the model to stop generating.

model_parameters.label: Stream
model_parameters.helpText: The stream parameter in the API controls whether the response is sent in incremental updates, like tokenized data as it's generated, or as a complete result in one go.

model_parameters.label: Presence Penalty
model_parameters.helpText: How much to penalize new tokens based on whether they appear in the text so far. Increases the model's likelihood to talk about new topics.

model_parameters.label: Stop
model_parameters.helpText: Custom text sequences that will cause the model to stop generating.

model_parameters.label: Web Search
model_parameters.helpText: Enable web search tool for real-time information retrieval.

model_parameters.label: Prompt Tools (Functions)
model_parameters.helpText: A list of tools the model may call.

model_parameters.label: Temperature
model_parameters.helpText: What sampling temperature to use, between 0 and 2. Higher values like 0.8 will make the output more random, while lower values like 0.2 will make it more focused and deterministic.

model_parameters.label: Max Tokens
model_parameters.helpText: The maximum number of tokens that can be generated in the Result.

model_parameters.label: Stop Sequence
model_parameters.helpText: Custom text sequences that will cause the model to stop generating.

model_parameters.label: Top K
model_parameters.helpText: The top_k parameter is used to limit the number of choices for the next predicted word or token. It specifies the maximum number of tokens to consider at each step, based on their probability of occurrence. This technique helps to speed up the generation process and can improve the quality of the generated text by focusing on the most likely options.

model_parameters.label: Stream
model_parameters.helpText: The stream parameter in the API controls whether the response is sent in incremental updates, like tokenized data as it's generated, or as a complete result in one go.

model_parameters.label: Metadata
model_parameters.helpText: An external identifier for the user who is associated with the request.

model_parameters.label: Prompt Tools (Functions)
model_parameters.helpText: A list of tools the model may call.

model_parameters.label: Temperature
model_parameters.helpText: What sampling temperature to use, between 0 and 2. Higher values like 0.8 will make the output more random, while lower values like 0.2 will make it more focused and deterministic.

model_parameters.label: Max Tokens
model_parameters.helpText: The maximum number of tokens that can be generated in the Result.

model_parameters.label: Stop
model_parameters.helpText: Custom text sequences that will cause the model to stop generating.

model_parameters.label: Top P
model_parameters.helpText: An alternative to sampling with temperature, called nucleus sampling, where the model considers the results of the tokens with top_p probability mass. So 0.1 means only the tokens comprising the top 10% probability mass are considered.

model_parameters.label: Frequency Penalty
model_parameters.helpText: Number between 0 and 2.0. Positive values penalize new tokens based on their existing frequency in the text so far, decreasing the model's likelihood to repeat the same line verbatim.

model_parameters.label: Logit Bias
model_parameters.helpText: Modify the likelihood of specified tokens appearing in the completion.

model_parameters.label: Logprobs
model_parameters.helpText: Whether to return log probabilities of the output tokens or not. If true, returns the log probabilities of each output token returned in the content of message.

model_parameters.label: Top Logprobs
model_parameters.helpText: An integer between 0 and 5 specifying the number of most likely tokens to return at each token position, each with an associated log probability.

model_parameters.label: Number of Responses
model_parameters.helpText: How many chat completion choices to generate for each input message.

model_parameters.label: Presence Penalty
model_parameters.helpText: Positive values penalize new tokens based on whether they appear in the text so far, increasing the model's likelihood to talk about new topics.

model_parameters.label: Seed
model_parameters.helpText: (BETA) - System will make a best effort to sample deterministically, such that repeated requests with the same seed and parameters should return the same result.

model_parameters.label: Stream
model_parameters.helpText: The stream parameter in the API controls whether the response is sent in incremental updates, like tokenized data as it's generated, or as a complete result in one go.

model_parameters.label: Metadata
model_parameters.helpText: A unique identifier representing your end-user.
